library_preparation_preflight.summary: org.apache.groovy:groovy-macro:4.0.24 only depends on org.apache.groovy:groovy:4.0.24 and is normally exercised in-process through Groovy compilation/macros; no Docker image, extra Maven dependency, environment variable, or system property is required before meaningful coverage is reachable.
library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: No environment or system properties are needed. Meaningful tests should exercise macro expansion through Groovy runtime APIs already available transitively, such as GroovyShell or GroovyClassLoader evaluating code that calls macro { ... }, rather than only instantiating classes.
risks: A scaffold-only Java test that merely loads macro classes would provide weak coverage; useful coverage requires compiling or evaluating Groovy code so the macro AST transformation and extension module are used.
If the generated project does not apply the Groovy Gradle plugin, keep test sources in Java and invoke GroovyShell/GroovyClassLoader from Java rather than adding Groovy test source files.
